_fixture_meta.label: positive_static_transfer_hook
_fixture_meta.notes: SYNTHETIC fixture — no publicly-flagged Solana token with a confirmed-malicious transfer hook was available in the analyst corpus-sourcing run (2026-04-21). The RugCheck API and Offside Security corpus (research/02-detection-methodology.md §Honeypot gap) contain transfer-hook tokens but none with a confirmed-rugged label that could be captured as a fixture at Sprint 2 exit. This synthetic fixture is constructed to exercise the S4 code path and calibrate the lower confidence boundary for a hook-only signal. REPLACEMENT INSTRUCTIONS: When a live confirmed-rugged token with transferHook set to a deployer-controlled program is documented in the wild (e.g., via RugCheck rugged=true + transferHook != null), replace this file with the captured on-chain state and remove the synthetic=true flag. Cite research/02-detection-methodology.md §Honeypot gap as the gap reference.

expected_confidence_walk.note: S4 alone produces low-end confidence. Only simulation (S6, Phase 3) can confirm whether the hook is malicious. This is the documented worst-case FN scenario from review §5.

creator: DEPLOYER_WALLET_PLACEHOLDER

risks.name: Transfer Hook Present
risks.description: Token has a Token-2022 transfer hook program set. The hook executes arbitrary logic on every transfer and can silently revert sell transactions for non-whitelisted addresses.

source_reference: SYNTHETIC — see _fixture_meta.notes. Research gap documented in research/02-detection-methodology.md §Honeypot gap.
